note: These ids are referenced in related_nodes of existing nodes but do not yet exist. They must be created (or remapped) to close the graph. No ontology content is defined here — only the dependency contract.

dangling_nodes.why_required: One of the five higher objectives (maqāṣid). The principle node 'nafs' carries the concept; this node is the explicit Maqasid-level preservation objective used as a top-tier protection test in decision analysis.

Maqasid-level objective protecting sound reason from impairment, manipulation, or intoxication. The faculty node 'aql' supplies the concept; this node anchors the preservation objective (critical for AI/manipulation analysis).

Maqasid-level objective protecting freedom of conviction and conscience. The concept node 'din' supplies the principle; this node anchors the preservation objective for governance and rights analysis.

dangling_nodes.proposed_name: Preservation of Progeny / Future Generations (Ḥifẓ an-Nasl)
dangling_nodes.why_required: Maqasid-level objective protecting lineage and the wellbeing of future generations. Referenced by both the concept node 'nasl' and the stewardship node 'khalifah', making it a key intergenerational node for climate and long-horizon analysis.

Maqasid-level objective protecting lawful property and economic security. The concept node 'mal' supplies the principle; this node anchors the preservation objective for finance, ESG, and anti-fraud analysis.

Referenced by 'ihsan' as the execution-quality counterpart to excellence: doing work with precision and mastery. Needed to support quality-of-build and engineering-integrity analysis (e.g. nuclear safety, AI system rigour) distinct from the broader ihsān concept.

summary.no_remaps_recommended: All six are genuine new nodes; none should be remapped onto existing ids. The five maqasid_hifz_* nodes form the Maqasid preservation tier (category 15) and should be created as a coherent set; 'itqan' is an independent virtue node.
